genre_refs: Battle of New Orleans (Edward Percy Moran painting); War of 1812 history
description: A reproduction of the early-20th-century history painting 'The Battle of New Orleans' by Edward Percy Moran (identified in the tweet text). The oil painting depicts the January 1815 battle: General Andrew Jackson stands atop an earthwork rampart at right, sword raised, in a dark blue uniform, the Stars and Stripes flying behind him; American defenders fire muskets from the parapet. At left and center, red-coated British soldiers and kilted Scottish Highland infantry climb scaling ladders against the fortifications, some falling wounded; a Union Jack and a fallen drum are visible in the foreground; smoke and a clouded sky fill the background, with ships faintly on the water at far left. The image is a reproduction of a painting with no added graphic-design elements, overlaid text, typography, slogans, or agency logo on the image itself. The Department of Labor caption includes the phrases 'Rugged American Spirit' and 'Remember who you are, American.'
notable_text: (no on-image text; the artwork is 'The Battle of New Orleans' by Edward Percy Moran per the tweet)
